sanskrit:
योगी युञ्जीत सततमात्मानं रहसि स्थितः।
एकाकी यतचित्तात्मा निराशीः परिग्रहः॥
transliteration: yogī yuñjīta satatam ātmānaṃ rahasi sthitaḥ | ekākī yata-cittātmā nirāśīr aparigrahaḥ ||
literal_meaning: The yogī should constantly unite the self, remaining in a secluded place, solitary, with controlled mind and self, free from longing and possession.
contextual_meaning: Sustained, solitary, non-attached focus is the royal road to inner stillness; external withdrawal plus internal relinquishment equals psychological freedom.
psychological_state: Deliberate self-contained attention that dissolves possessive craving through solitude and disciplined mind.
trigger_scenario: Turning off notifications, booking a solo weekend cabin, and noticing the itch to check your phone—realizing the itch is the practice.
anchor_text: Sit alone, breathe, let go—no wants, no things—just quiet mind.
